title: ISE-Cisco ACI / Cisco Secure Workload SGT Binding Synchronisation

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 1.4.4 requires NSC configurations be reviewed at least every six months. Sync-failure detection is operational evidence supporting the periodic review.
compliance.controlObjective: Operationally evidence NSC sync between ISE and ACI/Secure Workload enforcement.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.37 alert log + monthly sync-failure report.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(d) covers access control. SGT/EPG binding fidelity is a continuity input for east-west access enforcement.
compliance.controlObjective: Operational continuity of cross-system access enforcement.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.22 (Segregation of networks) — segmentation between ISE and ACI/Secure Workload must remain consistent across both planes; sync-failure detection evidences continuity.
compliance.controlObjective: Maintain consistency of segmentation across ISE and downstream enforcement.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab ACI fabric, delete an EPG that ISE has an active SGT-to-EPG binding for. ISE emits 83022 (binding update failed). UC-17.1.37 fires showing the (sgt, epg) pair with `failures > 0`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Recreate the EPG. ISE re-syncs and emits 83020 (binding created). UC-17.1.37 does not fire on the next schedule.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *TrustSec* and *pxGrid* categories — codes `83020` (SGT-EPG mapping created), `83021` (SGT-EPG mapping deleted), `83022` (SGT-EPG mapping update failed). The Cisco ACI APIC controller syslog `index=network sourcetype=cisco:apic` reports `epg_static_path_change` and `EPG-binding-failure` events. Cisco Secure Workload (formerly Tetration) reports its scope-binding via the OpenAPI `/openapi/v1/inventory_filters` polled by a custom modular input.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services` ([Splunkbase 1915](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1915)) + `Cisco ACI Connector` ([Splunkbase 4309](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4309)) + custom Secure Workload poller.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (83020, 83021, 83022))
  OR (index=network sourcetype="cisco:apic" event_type IN ("epg_static_path_change", "EPG-binding-failure"))
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event, count(eval(MessageCode=="83022" OR event_type=="EPG-binding-failure")) as failures by sgt_name, epg_name
| where failures > 0
| sort - failures
description: Detects SGT-to-EPG (Cisco ACI Endpoint Group) binding synchronisation failures and SGT-to-Secure-Workload-scope binding update failures — symptoms of integration drift between ISE TrustSec policy and Cisco ACI / Secure Workload enforcement.
value: When ISE's TrustSec policy and ACI/Secure Workload enforcement diverge, traffic that ISE classified as 'production' may be enforced by ACI as 'staging' (or vice versa). Catching binding-update failures in real time prevents silent classification drift.
implementation: Onboard ACI APIC syslog into `index=network`. Onboard Secure Workload binding events via the OpenAPI poller. Schedule the search every 30 min, search window `-1h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0.
visualization: (1) Single value: count of failed bindings last 24h. (2) Sankey: sgt_name → epg_name → status (success/failure). (3) Top-N table: SGTs with most failed bindings.

references.title: Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — TrustSec / ACI Integration

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Bulk ACI EPG renames** — A scheduled rename causes a transient binding miss until ACI re-resolves. Suppress within scheduled change windows. (b) **Secure Workload scope reorganisation** — Major scope-tree changes generate binding update activity that may transiently fail before the new tree is applied.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE-ACI integration configured (Administration > pxGrid Services > ACI Settings).
• ACI APIC syslog forwarded to Splunk.
• Splunk credentials for the Secure Workload OpenAPI; modular input or cron poller.
• `sgt_epg_mapping.csv` lookup (SGT → expected EPG).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable ISE *TrustSec* category at INFO. Verify ACI APIC syslog is arriving at `index=network sourcetype=cisco:apic`.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 30 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke a binding failure as in controlTest. Confirm fire.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard panels listed above. Slack notification to `#network-segmentation-platform`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No ACI syslog events** — APIC syslog target not configured. Configure under APIC: Admin > External Data Collectors > Syslog.
• **Binding-failure events from ISE without ACI events** — APIC may be reachable but Secure Workload integration broken. Validate Secure Workload OpenAPI auth.
• **Persistent failures for one SGT** — That SGT has no matching EPG defined in ACI. Coordinate with the ACI admin to create or rename the EPG.
grandmaExplanation: ISE labels users by group, but the data-centre fabric and the workload-protection tool need to know about the same groups. We make sure those tools agree with ISE — and ring the bell when one of them refuses a label, so security rules stay consistent.
